opportunities.title: Zero-Day Alert: CISA Confirms Active Exploitation of Joomla Extension Flaws — Patch iCagenda and Balbooa Forms Now
opportunities.why_now: CISA's KEV addition plus independent researcher confirmation of active zero-day exploitation makes this a today-not-tomorrow patch mandate.
opportunities.angle: Urgent patch-now advisory with CVE breakdown, affected versions, and exploitation timeline; cross-link CISA + Hacker News + SecurityWeek reporting for credibility

opportunities.title: Supply Chain Rot: Popular npm Package Jscrambler Backdoored With Rust Infostealer
opportunities.why_now: A widely-used JS obfuscation tool got compromised at v8.14.0, hitting dev pipelines directly — dual-sourced by Hacker News and BleepingComputer within the last 48 hours.
opportunities.angle: Incident breakdown: what jscrambler is, how the infostealer got in, IOCs, and a call for CI/CD dependency-pinning discipline

opportunities.title: Patch Tuesday, Any Day: RabbitMQ and Zimbra Critical RCEs Land in the Same News Cycle
opportunities.why_now: Two separate critical code-execution vulnerabilities in widely deployed enterprise messaging/collab software dropped the same day, doubling the patch-cadence urgency.
opportunities.angle: Side-by-side patch digest: CVSS scores, exploitability, who's affected, and a ranked action list for sysadmins

opportunities.title: Japan's Largest Taxi Operator Goes Dark: When Ransomware Hits Physical Infrastructure
opportunities.why_now: A major real-world operational shutdown from a cyberattack just happened, giving the 'inevitable breach' narrative a fresh, visceral example.
opportunities.angle: Breach postmortem angle — what's known so far, likely attack vector, and what it signals about critical-infrastructure resilience in transit sector

opportunities.title: CrashStealer Proves Notarization Isn't Trust: New macOS Malware Sails Past Gatekeeper
opportunities.why_now: A newly identified macOS infostealer is using a notarized dropper to bypass Apple's core security control, undermining a widely-trusted assumption.
opportunities.angle: Myth-busting explainer on Gatekeeper/notarization limits, IOC list, and why 'notarized' no longer means 'safe' for enterprise Mac fleets
